hero.subtitle: Medallion architecture with centralized governance. Comparative analysis between Microsoft Fabric and Open Source solution, focusing on time-to-value and scalability.

context.title: XPTO's Data Problem
context.subtitle: The organization accumulated over 50 disconnected source systems. Decision making depends on manual spreadsheets and ad-hoc low-reliability reports.

silos.title: Data Silos
silos.desc: 50+ disconnected legacy systems generate severe inconsistencies in management reporting.

quality.title: Degraded Quality
quality.desc: Without a quality pipeline, critical errors reach the end user, undermining trust.

governance.title: Absent Governance
governance.desc: Data lineage and access control are nonexistent in the current scenario.

time.title: Slow Time-to-Insight
time.desc: The gap between raw data and decision is measured in days. The dynamic market demands hours.

context.current_state_desc:
sources: [
  { name: "billing_ERP",    format: "SQL",  freq: "daily",    quality: "medium" },
  { name: "sales_CRM",      format: "API",  freq: "realtime", quality: "medium" },
  { name: "HR_spreadsheets", format: "xlsx", freq: "manual",   quality: "low" },
  // ... + 47 additional legacy sources
]
governance:   null  // ⚠️ no lineage
data_catalog: null  // ⚠️ no inventory

medallion.title: Medallion Architecture
medallion.subtitle: Separation of responsibilities in distinct layers, ensuring each zone has a clear and auditable quality contract, physically separating the Data Lake (Storage) from the Data Warehouse (Analytics).

star_schema.title: Dimensional Modeling: Star Schema vs. Snowflake
star_schema.subtitle: Why does the Gold layer use Star Schema (Denormalized) modeling?
star_schema.perf_desc: Drastically minimizes complex JOINs in analytical queries. Data is pre-consolidated into directly connected fact and dimension tables.
star_schema.usability_desc: Intuitive structure for analysts and business users. Easy to drag and drop fields in visualization tools (Power BI/Superset).
star_schema.maint_desc: Facilitates the creation of consistent, consolidated DAX/SQL measures. Single, centralized business aggregation rules on the right dimensions.

decision.title: Microsoft Fabric vs. Open Source
decision.subtitle: Weighted comparative analysis. The final recommendation considers XPTO's context: lean team, existing M365 ecosystem, and high delivery urgency.
decision.rec_desc: Given the presence of the M365 ecosystem, the priority in governance, and the business-required time-to-value, Microsoft Fabric offers the fastest and safest route. OneLake architecture eliminates storage duplication, and Purview resolves compliance natively.

fabric_page.title: Microsoft Fabric Architecture
fabric_page.subtitle: Unified analytical platform built on OneLake. A single SaaS license, zero storage silos, native governance, and full integration with XPTO's M365 ecosystem.
fabric_page.comp_onelake_desc: Centralized storage for all organizational data. Eliminates storage silos and allows multi-workload access to the same data without copy.
fabric_page.comp_purview_desc: Automatic data catalog, end-to-end data lineage, sensitive data classification, and policy-based access control. Native compliance.
fabric_page.comp_pbi_desc: Interactive reports with Direct Lake mode (queries straight on Analysis Services RAM on OneLake) without data replication and with sub-second response.
fabric_page.comp_df_desc: Low-code orchestration pipelines with native connectors for XPTO's 50+ sources. Real-time CDC (Change Data Capture) support.
fabric_page.comp_synapse_desc: Distributed processing engine for complex transformations in Bronze/Silver layers. Supports Spark, T-SQL, and Python.
fabric_page.comp_sec_desc: Managed network isolation for workloads. Private endpoints and dedicated IPs ensure data travels in encrypted private channels.
fabric_page.sec_gov_desc: With Microsoft Fabric, Row-Level Security (RLS) is configured centrally in the SQL Endpoint/Warehouse. A single security policy restricts data for Power BI reports and direct T-SQL queries, ensuring compliance without logic replication.
fabric_page.sec_gov_code_title:
-- RLS Example in Fabric Warehouse:
CREATE SECURITY POLICY RegionSecurityPolicy
ADD FILTER PREDICATE Security.fn_securitypredicate(Region_Key)
ON Gold.FACT_SALES WITH (STATE = ON);
fabric_page.timeline_1_desc: VNet and OneLake configuration. Initial ingestion of priority legacy sources via Data Factory.
fabric_page.timeline_2_desc: Type normalization in Bronze. Automatic schema cataloging in Purview.
fabric_page.timeline_3_desc: Joining client and billing data in Silver. Application of auditing rules.
fabric_page.timeline_4_desc: Star Schema modeling in Gold, implementation of RLS policies, and delivery of Power BI dashboards.
fabric_page.cta_sub: Compare with the Open Source alternative

opensource_page.title: Open Source Architecture
opensource_page.subtitle: Stack built on renowned open source components. Maximum operational control and customization, with reduced licensing costs — but with high management complexity.
opensource_page.comp_airflow_desc: DAG-based workflow orchestration. Massive flexibility with Python to orchestrate ingestion pipelines of the 50+ sources, but requires infra maintenance.
opensource_page.comp_spark_desc: Distributed engine for data transformations and processing in staging layers. High performance handling parquet/delta data.
opensource_page.comp_minio_desc: High-performance object storage with standard S3 APIs. Used as a private data lake running on dedicated instances.
opensource_page.comp_dbt_desc: Orchestrates and tests SQL transformations inside the warehouse. Converts clean data into documented and tested fact and dimension tables.
opensource_page.comp_superset_desc: Modern and visual data exploration platform connected via Trino/PostgreSQL. Fast, scalable, and free of licensing costs.
opensource_page.comp_gov_desc: Enterprise metadata catalog (OpenMetadata) combined with robust role-based access control and RLS policies.
opensource_page.sec_gov_desc: In the open source stack, RLS security is applied directly at the database (PostgreSQL/Trino) or logically in the visualization layer of Apache Superset. Although flexible, it requires security rules to be manually replicated and configured in each consumption tool.
pros: No proprietary software licensing fees (zero vendor lock-in).
Full control over code, updates, and physical hosting.
Strong ecosystem with widely available market skills (Python/SQL).
High flexibility to plug in new ad-hoc governance tools.
cons: Extended Time-to-Value (4 to 8 weeks just for stable setup).
High operational burden in DevOps and Connectivity Engineering.
Complex manual integration between governance, cataloging, and storage.
Limited self-service BI experience compared to the Office ecosystem.
opensource_page.alt_scenario_title: When it makes sense to choose Open Source
opensource_page.alt_scenario_desc: If XPTO has strict regulatory compliance constraints that require data exclusively on-premises (absolute sovereignty), or already has a dedicated DevOps team experienced in the Linux/Docker stack, the open source solution becomes financially viable in the long run.
opensource_page.footer: Technical Case XPTO — Open Source Alternative
